name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Richmond, Va
description_short: NACA is a non-profit housing counselor providing character-based mortgages with no down payment, no closing costs, and no credit score consideration to low-to-moderate income borrowers.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union, growing from the first-ever housing trust fund negotiation into a national non-profit organization. Over 36 years, NACA has served 3 million people and helped 500,000 become homeowners, making it one of the largest HUD-certified housing counseling providers in America, responsible for 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally.

NACA offers a proprietary mortgage product called the "Best in America Mortgage" featuring no down payment, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance requirements, and no credit score consideration. Instead of credit-based underwriting, NACA uses character-based lending that evaluates a borrower's full circumstances. Current fixed rates (as of April 2026) are 5.625% for 30-year, 5.125% for 20-year, and 5% for 15-year mortgages, positioned below market rates. The organization has made $20 billion in mortgage commitments and currently holds approximately 75,000 active mortgages. NACA also provides housing counseling and advocacy services, including campaigns against predatory landlords and Wall Street real estate investors.

NACA distinguishes itself through its explicit focus on closing the racial wealth disparity gap and serving historically underserved communities, particularly low-to-moderate-income people and people of color. Their character-based lending model removes traditional credit score barriers that disproportionately affect these populations. The organization operates on a mission-driven, non-profit model rather than profit maximization, with revenue directed back into member services and advocacy. NACA hosts multi-day "Achieve the Dream Events" nationwide where prospective members can access counseling, meet staff, and begin the qualification process.

However, potential borrowers should understand that NACA membership requires active participation—the organization emphasizes member engagement and volunteer opportunities as part of its community-based model. The "no credit score consideration" advantage applies only to NACA's proprietary mortgage; this is not a mainstream lending product available through traditional banks. Approval requires meeting NACA's qualification standards and attending their events or counseling sessions. Their focus on advocacy and activism, while aligned with their mission, means this is fundamentally different from a conventional lender.
pros: No down payment required—removes largest barrier to homeownership for low-income buyers
No closing costs or fees—eliminates thousands of dollars in typical mortgage expenses
No credit score consideration—uses character-based underwriting instead of credit history
Below-market fixed rates—5.625% (30-yr) positioned competitively against conventional mortgages
No mortgage insurance—saves borrowers 0.5-1% annually on monthly payments
HUD-certified housing counselor—30% of all HUD housing counseling services provided
Non-profit model—mission-driven organization with $20B in mortgage commitments
cons: Requires active member participation and engagement—not a passive consumer relationship
Limited geographic availability dependent on Achieve the Dream Events schedule
Proprietary product only available through NACA—cannot shop rates with competitors
Character-based lending still has qualification requirements not detailed on homepage
Activist/advocacy focus means organization prioritizes social mission over convenience
best_for: Low-to-moderate-income first-time homebuyers with poor or no credit history
Borrowers of color and historically underserved communities facing credit discrimination
People seeking below-market mortgage rates without down payment or closing costs
Individuals willing to engage in community participation and advocacy
diagnosis: NACA is best for low-to-moderate-income first-time homebuyers and communities of color who lack down payment savings or have credit challenges, provided they can commit to attending multi-day events and engaging with the organization's community-based model. The main caveat is that this is a non-profit with a social mission, not a conventional lender—approval requires character-based qualification, active participation, and attendance at their regional events.
services: Character-based mortgage origination (no down payment, no closing costs, no PMI)
HUD-approved housing counseling and qualification assistance
Multi-day Achieve the Dream Events in multiple cities nationwide
Member portal for document upload and application status tracking
Real estate agent network and referral services
Home inspector coordination and partnership services
Settlement agent and title services network
Predatory landlord advocacy and tenant support
Community advocacy campaigns against corporate real estate investors
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1600 Brook Rd Ste C, Richmond, VA 23220
